name: Social cross-posting / multi-platform posting
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Social cross-posting / multi-platform posting

CONNECTING PLATFORMS (REQUIRED):
The app MUST include a way for users to "connect" or "link" each platform before they can post. Do not only show platform selection toggles—add one of:
1. A **Settings** or **Connected accounts** screen (e.g. from a gear or profile icon) where each platform has a row: platform name, connection status ("Not connected" / "Connected"), and a "Connect" or "Add account" button. Tapping it can show an alert or sheet explaining "In a full app you would sign in with [platform] here; this demo saves your choice." and then mark the platform as connected for the session.
2. On the main compose screen, make each platform row tappable: first tap = "Connect" flow (alert/sheet), second tap = toggle selected for this post. Show a small indicator (checkmark or "Connected") so users see which accounts are linked.

Real APIs (Twitter/X, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Mastodon, Bluesky) each require OAuth or API keys; you cannot implement real posting without backend and app registration. So:
- Implement **connection UI** (Connect / Add account) and **connection state** (connected vs not) so the flow is clear.
- For the actual "Post" action, either simulate success (e.g. "Posted to 3 platforms" alert) or show "Would post to X, Y, Z (demo mode)".
- Optionally add a short in-app note: "Connect your accounts in Settings. Real posting requires API setup per platform."

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT only offer platform toggles with no way to "connect" or "add account".
- Do NOT claim the app actually posts to live APIs without implementing OAuth/API keys (use demo/simulated posting).
antiPatterns: Only platform selection toggles with no Connect / Add account flow
Claiming real posting to live APIs without OAuth/backend
